brands.name: Muji
brands.q1: We make everyday products stripped of everything unnecessary.
brands.q2: Products are designed to impress in the shop, not to last in your home.
brands.q3: She lives in a small flat that feels cluttered. She wants fewer things, better made. Nothing she owns quite fits.
brands.gap: The only brand that made no-brand a philosophy, not a budget choice.
brands.purpose: For people who are tired of being sold to. They deserve products that respect their intelligence and their space.

brands.name: Oatly
brands.tagline: It's like milk but made for humans.
brands.q1: We make oat-based food for people who want milk without the cow.
brands.q2: The food industry spends more energy on marketing health than actually being healthy.
brands.q3: He cares about what he puts in his body but finds every alternative tastes like compromise.
brands.gap: The only plant-based brand that refuses to pretend it isn't trying to change the world.
brands.purpose: For people who want to make better choices but won't sacrifice taste or be lectured to.

brands.name: ColorFlix
brands.tagline: Colour for those who refuse to blend in.
brands.q1: We make bold colour cosmetics for Gen Z women who refuse to blend in.
brands.q2: Beauty brands tell Indian women their natural skin tone needs correcting.
brands.q3: She is 19, experimental, broke, and the beauty aisle was never designed for her skin or her budget.
brands.gap: The only Indian cosmetics brand that celebrates what you look like instead of fixing it.
brands.purpose: For young Indian women who want to experiment with colour without being told they need to look a certain way first.

brands.name: Paper Boat
brands.q1: We make drinks that taste like the ones your grandmother made.
brands.q2: Packaged drinks erased the taste of real Indian ingredients and called it progress.
brands.q3: He is 35 and misses the taste of his childhood. Nothing he finds in a store comes close.
brands.gap: The only beverage brand that treats memory as an ingredient.
brands.purpose: For anyone who has lost the taste of something they grew up with and wants it back.

brands.name: Zepto
brands.q1: We deliver groceries in ten minutes so your day doesn't stop.
brands.q2: Grocery shopping was designed around the store's convenience, not yours.
brands.q3: She is a working mother at 7pm realising dinner needs one ingredient she doesn't have. Time is the thing that's missing.
brands.gap: The only grocery service built around the moment you realise you need something, not the moment you planned to shop.
brands.purpose: For people whose time is the most valuable thing they have and who refuse to waste it on errands.

brands.name: Frida Kahlo
brands.q1: I paint my reality so that the world cannot look away from what it refuses to see.
brands.q2: The art world demanded women paint beauty. I refused to hide the pain.
brands.q3: She is anyone who has been told her story is too small, too female, too brown to matter. What's missing is a mirror.
brands.gap: The only artist who made personal suffering a universal language.
brands.purpose: For anyone whose truth has been called too much — and who needs proof that it is exactly enough.

brands.name: Basquiat
brands.tagline: The crown was never theirs to give.
brands.q1: I make art that puts Black identity and street knowledge where the establishment said it didn't belong.
brands.q2: The art world wanted to own Black creativity without crediting or paying the people it came from.
brands.q3: He is a young Black artist in a city that tells him his culture is entertainment but not fine art. What's missing is legitimacy on his own terms.
brands.gap: The only artist who forced fine art to reckon with what it had stolen.
brands.purpose: For anyone whose culture has been appropriated, diluted, and sold back to them at a premium.

brands.name: Niharika NM
brands.tagline: The voice you were told to keep quiet.
brands.q1: I make comedy that sounds exactly like the voice in every South Indian girl's head that she was told to keep quiet.
brands.q2: Influencer culture rewards polish and aspiration. I built an audience by being the opposite of both.
brands.q3: She is 22, from Chennai or Bangalore, consumes English content but lives a very Indian life. What's missing is someone who gets both without explaining either.
brands.gap: The only Indian creator who made being unapologetically herself a full creative identity — not a personality quirk.
brands.purpose: For young Indian women who consume global content but live very Indian lives and have never seen themselves represented without apology.
